copy.caption: The ocean's top team player — hunting together like a wolf pack beneath the waves.
copy.shortDescription: Orcas are the largest dolphins in the world, and they're one of the only animals known to intentionally beach themselves briefly to snatch seals from the shore — a trick mothers teach their calves by practicing on safe beaches with shallow water. Different orca families have their own dialects, passed down from grandmothers to grandchildren. Fun fact: An orca family's calls are so distinct they work like a language only that pod understands — and researchers can identify a pod just by listening.
